name: Pork & Chile Burritos
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Boneless lean pork, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1/2 cup Cornstarch blended with 1 cup Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the pork in a Dutch oven. Add 1 gallon of water and bring to boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, until meat is half-cooked, about 35 minutes. Drain off water.
3. Add onions, bell peppers, cilantro, tomatoes, garlic powder, salt, cumin, cloves, pepper, bay leaves, and jalapeno chile to the meat and simmer.
4. Remove husks from tomatillos and boil in a saucepan in 1 quart of water until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain off water.
5. Mash the tomatillos and add them to the meat mixture. Simmer, stirring constantly for 15 minutes.
6. Add lemon juice. Stir the cornstarch mixture then stir into the meat mixture and simmer for 15 minutes longer, stirring constantly.
7. Heat tortillas gently until softened (heat one at a time directly on burner of range or wrap in foil and place in oven preheated to 350°F for a few minutes).
8. To make burritos, place a heaping 1/2 cup of meat mixture on each tortilla. Fold sides in, then roll up from one end.
9. Sprinkle burritos generously with cheese and place under broiler just until cheese melts.
